analyst_observations: Eval 0 is non-discriminating: both configs scored 7/7. The baseline had access to the codebase (17 tool calls vs 9 for with-skill) which explains parity — in a context without the project files, the baseline would likely fail several assertions.

The real discriminating value of the skill shows in evals 1 and 2: baseline failed on runic-specific details (wrong --dry-run flag, hallucinated MigrationContext import/signature) while with-skill was 100% correct.
With-skill used 16% fewer tokens overall despite the skill file loading, because it needed fewer exploratory tool calls.

Eval 2 baseline failure is notable: it hallucinated `from runic import MigrationContext` — a non-existent import — and used the wrong function signature `def upgrade(context: MigrationContext)`. The skill cleanly prevents this.
